items.title: CIT chief told she could be fired for disclosing misconduct probe

children.content: Margot McNeill did not tell the Canberra Institute of Technology board she was under investigation for misconduct when she was being recruited.

image.altText: A professional in front of CIT signage

items.title: Canberra teen charged with planning terrorist attack

children.content: The 17-year-old appeared in the ACT Children's Court today charged with one count of planning or preparing for a terrorist act and one count of transmitting violent extremist material.

image.altText: ACT Magistrate Court

items.title: Federal budget includes $50 million to duplicate Belconnen road

children.content: Other funding boosts for the ACT revealed in the budget papers include $50 million to duplicate Drake Brockman Drive in West Belconnen, aimed at servicing the area's growing population, and almost $10 million for the National Film and Sound Archive.

gallery.altText: An aerial view of a collection of high-rise buildings, with a lake in the foreground and Telstra Tower in the background.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial view of a collection of high-rise buildings, with a lake in the foreground and Telstra Tower in the background.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: Australian paratrooper killed after mid-air collision during training

children.content: Warrant Officer Class Two Lachlan Muddle, a highly qualified special forces sniper and military free-fall parachutist, has died after a mid-air collision with another paratrooper during an army training exercise.

image.altText: A man poses seriously wearing army uniform, in front of an Australian flag.

items.title: ADFA cadet arrested, charged after hidden camera found in bathroom

children.content: An Australian Defence Force Academy cadet who allegedly used a camera hidden in a soap dispenser to film female cadets in the bathroom has been granted bail in the ACT Magistrates Court.

image.altText: A man with short hair wearing a t-shirt walks out of a court building.

items.title: Four more ANU council members quit after Julie Bishop's shock resignation

children.content: The acting chancellor of the Australian National University confirms four more members of the university council have resigned, following last week's exit of former chancellor Julie Bishop.

image.altText: Signage for the ANU.

items.title: $100m bid to reduce Canberra to Sydney train travel time to under 4 hours

children.content: The federal, ACT and NSW governments will spend $100 million upgrading the Canberra to Sydney rail link in a bid to reduce the train trip to under four hours.

image.altText: A train sitting at a train platform with people preparing to board.

items.title: 'Leaks in the urinals': Political journalism's backstory flushed out at Old Parliament House

children.content: The new Press Gallery exhibition takes visitors inside the original working spaces at Old Parliament House where reporters worked between 1927 and 1988.

gallery.altText: Malcom Fraser walking outside Old Parliament House surrounded by a crowd.
The words 'The Press Gallery' on a wall in the entrance to an exhibition.
An office filled with papers and a typewriter.

thumbnail.altText: Malcom Fraser walking outside Old Parliament House surrounded by a crowd.

Trump and Xi walk down a red carpet
Xi and Trump stand on stairs in Beijing, Xi

articles.title: Only one leader has the upper hand in Trump-Xi China meeting

children.content: Nine years after his last meeting with Xi Jinping in China, Donald Trump is more aggressive and assertive. But so is Beijing. And its president is ready to take the geopolitical and economic lead.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping react as they hold a bilateral meeting.
Trump walks with Xi Jinping alongside a column of Chinese troops in uniform

children.content: The war in Iran, Taiwan, trade, tariffs and AI could all feature in Donald Trump's first visit to Beijing in nearly nine years. Follow live.

items.title: Donald Trump tells Xi Jinping they will have 'fantastic future' in bilateral meeting

updates.title: How do Chinese people feel about Trump's visit?
Xi says world has reached a 'crossroads'

items.title: Angus Taylor proposes one-for-one migration cap tied to housing

children.content: The Coalition wants to limit Australia's net migration intake to only one person per new home built, but would wait until in government to decide what visa types to slash if elected.

image.altText: A man in a suit extends his hand in exclamation while speaking to a group of journalists.

items.title: UAE denies meeting with Netanyahu, Iran warns against neighbours 'colluding' with Israel

children.content: Donald Trump is expected to ask for China's help to resolve the costly and unpopular conflict with Iran, but analysts say he is unlikely to get the support he wants. Follow live.

image.altText: Benjamin Netanyahu at a press conference in front of an Israeli flag.

items.title: Sexual abuse, a thwarted Olympic dream, and the sister who uncovered the truth

children.content: Ian Walker was one of the most promising athletes in the country until his life started to unravel, and the search for the truth in his tragic story has finally come to light.

gallery.altText: An older image of three young siblings arm in arm smiling at each other.
A young man wearing black pants and a blue jumper stands smiling beneath a poster of Jim Morrison.

thumbnail.altText: An older image of three young siblings arm in arm smiling at each other.
A young man wearing black pants and a blue jumper stands smiling beneath a poster of Jim Morrison.

items.title: Australians 'literally dying' while they wait months for aged care packages

children.content: Opposition MPs accuse the federal government of burying figures showing Australians are waiting a year for residential aged care and at-home support packages.

image.altText: A bed in a resident's room in an aged care home.

items.title: Alex Murdaugh murder convictions overturned

children.content: An infamous case will be retried after a US court ruled in favour of the South Carolina father.

image.altText: a man in orange jumpsuit and glasses clasps hands listening in court

items.title: Voss exit shuts door on Carlton's premiership era that never was

children.content: Michael Voss leaves Carlton having taken the club to places it has rarely been in a quarter of a century, but still adrift from where it ultimately wants to be.

gallery.altText: Michael Voss with both fists raised in celebration

thumbnail.altText: Michael Voss with both fists raised in celebration

items.title: Autistic teenager handed supervision order for terror offences

children.content: A years-long saga that began with a 13-year-old being "radicalised" during a covert police operation is set to escalate to a Federal Court battle.

image.altText: Thomas's mother and father hold hands. Their faces are not seen in the photo

items.title: What we know about the NT government's plan to change child protection laws

children.content: Here's what you need to know about the proposed changes to the NT child protection legislation, introduced into parliament on Wednesday.

image.altText: A pink mural on a brick wall of a child holding an adult's hand.

items.title: Which country has won Eurovision the most? Take our More or Less quiz

children.content: We've put down our tiny flags and picked up our record books to challenge what you think you might know about Eurovision.

items.title: Anne Hathaway and Michaela Coel will bewitch you in Mother Mary

children.content: Director David Lowery has crafted a dark and mysterious chamber piece that turns a pop star's spiralling crisis into something supernatural.

image.altText: Mother Mary header

items.title: Blockbuster movies are connecting new audiences to classical music

children.content: From 19th-century concert traditions to Hollywood’s Golden Age, and back to the concert hall, movies are helping audiences experience live orchestral music.

image.altText: animation of young boy with brown hair with black dragon

items.title: King opens UK parliament but doomed PM Starmer may not last the week

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer's future as Britain's prime minister remains on life support amid mounting speculation he could face a leadership challenge as early as Thursday, local time.

items.title: Economists divided on extent of property price hit from budget tax overhaul

children.content: Some economists are forecasting price falls of up to 5 per cent as a result of measures to reduce intergenerational housing inequality.

image.altText: A house is advertised for auction in the south-west Sydney suburb of Punchbowl.

items.title: Almost 900 workers evacuated after chemical spill at meat processing plant

children.content: Five people were hospitalised after the liquid ammonia spill occurred just after midnight at a meat processing facility in Ipswich.

image.altText: Emergency crews respond to a chemical spill at a meat processing plant in Ipswich, May 14 2026.

items.title: Former Qld premier's partner Reza Adib's rape charges mentioned in court

children.content: A court hearing has been held for Vahid Reza Adib, the partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, for the first time since he was charged with rape, deprivation of liberty and sexual assault earlier this month.

image.altText: Gastric surgeon Reza Adib with Queensland Premier Annastacia Palaszczuk.

items.title: Sydney real estate agent fined $35k for falsely evicting tenant

children.content: One Sydney real estate agent was been fined $35,000 for evicting a tenant on the grounds that the landlord's relative wanted to live in the property, only they never moved in.

image.altText: A black and white for lease sign woth man in front

items.title: Residents fear houses could fall into the ocean and nothing is being done

children.content: Residents living on an idyllic stretch of the NSW far south coast are demanding action to protect their homes from erosion.

gallery.altText: storm debris at the back of a house

thumbnail.altText: storm debris at the back of a house

items.title: Canvas developers could be added to 'sucker list' after deal with hackers

children.content: The company that runs Canvas has painted a target on its back for future extortion attempts by making a deal with hackers, according to cybersecurity experts.

image.altText: a man sitting at a computer

items.title: Former Australian cricketer named first foreign English selector

children.content: The appointment of its first Australian selector, its first female coach and a new-look team has made it a landmark day for England's men's cricket team as it prepares to plunge into its post-Ashes Test recovery.

image.altText: Batter raising his creicket bat to acknowledge the crowd

items.title: Endangered frogs no bigger than a paper clip released into the wild

children.content: More than 800 tiny, technicolour northern corroboree frogs have been released into the wild to try to bolster the numbers of one of Australia's most critically endangered amphibians.

gallery.altText: An up close of the frogs, with black and yellow markings, in Del Leong's palm.
The frog, held between two human fingers, has black and yellow markings.
An up close of the Northern Corroboree frog, with black and yellow markings, in an enclosure.

The frog, held between two human fingers, has black and yellow markings.
An up close of the Northern Corroboree frog, with black and yellow markings, in an enclosure.

items.title: Child sex offender resigns as Melbourne Karen Buddhist group president

children.content: A convicted child sex offender who was told he was exempt from requiring a Working With Children Check has resigned as the president of a community organisation.

image.altText: An elderly man wearing a green jacket speaking into a microphone.

items.title: Fire at North Adelaide Golf Course suspicious, police say

children.content: Police say a fire destroyed a temporary structure often used at building sites and are calling for any witnesses who saw suspicious activity to come forward.

image.altText: A temporary building at a building site gutted by fire

items.title: Shots fired in Philippine senate as ICC seeks to arrest ex-police chief

children.content: Authorities are still trying to work out who fired the gun shots inside the senate building in the Philippines, prompting people to run for cover.

image.altText: Silhouettes of armed people in tactical gear in a parliament corridor

gallery.altText: People with guns inside a parliament building.

thumbnail.altText: People with guns inside a parliament building.

items.title: Oldest evidence of dentistry found in 59,000-year-old Neanderthal tooth

children.content: Archaeologists have found a hole in a 59,000-year-old tooth, which they say was drilled to treat a painful cavity. The find suggests Neanderthals could perform complex medical procedures.

image.altText: Museum diorama of Neanderthal family in a cave.

items.title: After long absence, Tasmania's short-stay figures just got released

children.content: Almost 500 dwellings in Tasmania have been converted to short-stay accommodation in just over a year. It's the first time short-stay data has been updated since the end of 2024.

image.altText: Woman opens a door with a key

items.title: Public memorial for James Valentine to be held in Sydney

children.content: Sydney Town Hall will host a memorial for the late radio presenter and musician, with limited spots available for members of the public.

image.altText: a man smiles while holding his chin
